text: The text and meaning of this verse is doubtful.
vv. 42-44. It can hardly be doubted that the Hebrew text as we have it is corrupt; no sense can be made out of it. The translation given above follows the Latin, which is somewhat less obscure.

This may refer (by a metaphor) to the Chaldaeans, but more probably, in these concluding verses, the coming fate of Jerusalem is left out of sight, and appeal is made to the moral sense of the prophet’s audience.
